transcript.clean_text: the fearsome 22 kilometers have finished the stage the Jayco herald-sun tour should see its first shuffle of the general classification today is the peloton travels a hundred and seventeen point six kilometers from Beechworth to Falls Creek the first half of the final ascent is undulating before a solid uphill grind to the last 15 kilometers with the steepest sections towards the top let's find out who conquered the creep after a flurry of early attacks it was yesterday's most combat evader ben hill alongside angus Lyons of Oliver's real food racing making the early running 20 kilometers in and it seemed as though we were set for the day but a five variety group behind were on their way to join the Giro Genki yamamoto of keen on cycling team Bentley Nick wey olden of Nero Continental Luke mud way of black smoke and the AVO loader of Kade Bickmore and Scott McGill made the junction after about 30 kilometers with a lead of three minutes over the peloton at this point we're bonuses on offer at the first intermediate sprint after 47 kilometers we saw the young American Bickmore get is we're in front of mud way Lyons didn't want any part of that sprint and that's because he had bigger fish to fry attacking the leaders with 55 kilometers still to run Ben Hill though doggedly determined as ever hung with him until the second intermediate sprint in the foot of the final climb before eventually waving goodbye to Lyons as we started that final ascent would be a tough ask for the 24 year old at this point now with only just over a minute advantage over the Sun Webb led peloton we did lose live pictures for a while but once we returned we saw fresh attacks from the bunch in the form of Salvador Guardiola of Keenan the peloton now much reduced with nearly half the climb in the legs mitchellson Scott now hitting the front and mopping up both lines and Guardiola with 13 kilometers to go next came a popular attack from EF pro cycling is Lachlan Morton a man who's won plenty of fans after racing dirty Kansa the Leadville 100 and GB Giro last year to be doing no harm to his fitness he was crushing a big gear up these slopes but it wouldn't be long before he had company J vine of Nero continental and the bunch not far down the hill and surprisingly this was the point where we saw simon yates shelled out the back the vaulter Espania winner was tattooed by many to be a stage favorite but he wouldn't be his day-to-day at least vine and martin were both reeled in by the group of favourites controlled by sun went Michael's Stora as more and more riders started to lose grounds number 17 here is Jimmy Whelan of EF pro cycling Hindley made his move after Stora peeled off of 2.5 to go only a handful of riders left at this point including Nilsson powerless Damien housen and roc power the only non World Tour rider in this group is the mightily impressive Sebastian Burak riding for Cink George Continental and he was giving them all a run for their money in the finale ultimately dropping power and rendering Paulus powerless he was Barrett who led out the sprint for the line but it was hidden Lee who was out of the settle and a little bit more springy on the pedals he held off a lunch to the line from house and to make it two wins into four teams some web and drawing first blood in the fight for the yellow jersey as we look at the top 10 we can see massive time gaps already Paulus limiting his losses in four for 13 seconds with Stora in fifth and Jessie youatt in sixth the only riders to finish within a minute of the stage winner house and in Henley look to reignite that friendly rivalry from the 2017 herald-sun tour this time its Hindley that takes the win on top of Falls Creek to take a slender lead on GC thanks to bonus seconds and how about Sebastian barek have we just uncovered the next great australian talent today only time will tell as Jayco herald-sun tour continues tomorrow for stage 3 as usual you can catch all the action right here on GGN racing you
